name: episki AI Governance
description: episki's plain-language AI governance control set: asset management, data protection, third-party AI risk, AI-specific risk, and program governance — crosswalked to NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, the EU AI Act, NIST CSF, ISO 27001, SOC 2, and PCI DSS.

controls.label: AI Asset Management
AI asset inventory is maintained and current
controls.description: A centralized inventory of AI tools, models, and APIs in use across the organization is maintained, classified, and kept current as new tools are adopted.

controls.label: New AI tools undergo governance review before adoption
controls.description: New AI tools are assessed for data sensitivity, deployment context, and regulatory applicability before they are approved for use; high-risk tools require documented evidence before activation.

controls.label: Deprecated AI tools are decommissioned with evidence
controls.description: AI tools that are retired or replaced are decommissioned through a documented process, with access revoked and data disposition recorded.

controls.label: Data Protection in AI
Data classification is documented for all AI assets
controls.description: For every AI asset, the categories of data it processes (PII, financial, health, proprietary, public) and the direction of data flow are documented.

controls.label: Data processing agreements are in place and current
controls.description: A signed data processing agreement is in place with each AI provider that handles regulated or customer data, and is reviewed on a defined cadence.

controls.label: Training opt-out is confirmed where applicable
controls.description: Where a provider may use inputs or outputs to train models, the organization has confirmed and recorded an opt-out (or accepted the residual risk explicitly).

controls.label: Data residency requirements are met per jurisdiction
controls.description: The geographic regions where each AI provider processes and stores data are documented and verified against the organization's data residency obligations.

controls.label: Third-Party AI Risk
Provider security certifications are verified annually
controls.description: Each AI provider's security certifications (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, etc.) are obtained and re-verified at least annually.

controls.label: Sub-processor lists are reviewed on provider update
controls.description: The sub-processors each AI provider relies on are tracked and re-reviewed whenever the provider updates its sub-processor list.

controls.label: Provider incident response obligations are documented
controls.description: Each AI provider's breach-notification and incident-response obligations are documented and aligned with the organization's own incident response plan.

controls.label: Provider terms of service changes are monitored
controls.description: Changes to AI providers' terms of service, acceptable use, and data-handling policies are monitored, and material changes trigger re-review.

controls.label: AI-Specific Risk
AI outputs are not used for automated decisions on individuals without human review
controls.description: AI systems are not permitted to make legal or similarly significant automated decisions about individuals without meaningful human review.

controls.label: Bias and fairness considerations are documented for customer-facing AI
controls.description: For customer-facing AI, bias and fairness risks are assessed and the assessment is documented and reviewed as models change.

controls.label: AI system failures have defined fallback procedures
controls.description: AI systems that support business processes have documented fallback procedures for when the AI is unavailable, degraded, or produces unreliable output.

controls.label: EU AI Act risk classification is assessed where applicable
controls.description: For organizations subject to the EU AI Act, each AI system is classified (prohibited, high-risk, limited, minimal) and the classification is documented.

controls.label: AI Governance Program
AI acceptable use policy is published and acknowledged
controls.description: An AI acceptable use policy is published, communicated, and acknowledged by personnel, and reviewed at least annually.

controls.label: AI governance roles and responsibilities are assigned
controls.description: Accountability for AI governance is assigned to named roles, with defined responsibilities for approval, oversight, and review.

controls.label: AI usage is included in security awareness training
controls.description: Personnel receive training on the safe and compliant use of AI tools as part of the security awareness program.

controls.label: AI governance posture is reported to leadership periodically
controls.description: The AI governance posture — inventory, risk, and control status — is reported to leadership on a defined cadence.
